hot_topics.topic: OpenAI's Latest AI Developments and Legal Challenges
hot_topics.synthesis: OpenAI has launched new products like "ChatGPT Work" for automating tasks and the GPT-5.6 family of models with advanced agentic capabilities, while also facing a legal challenge from The New York Times over alleged copyright infringement and evidence concealment. Concurrently, Instagram's head weighed in on AI content, suggesting users should be informed and given control over its presence in their feeds, and Meta introduced its Muse Spark 1.1 model for AI coding. These developments highlight both rapid innovation and growing legal and ethical scrutiny in the AI sector.

hot_topics.topic: EU Targets Social Media's Addictive Features
hot_topics.synthesis: The European Union is threatening Meta with fines, alleging that features on Facebook and Instagram, such as infinite scroll, autoplay, push notifications, and personalized recommendation algorithms, breach the Digital Services Act (DSA). The European Commission states these features are designed to exploit user vulnerabilities and foster addictive behaviors. This action underscores the EU's ongoing efforts to regulate large tech platforms and protect users from potentially harmful design choices.

hot_topics.topic: Political Reactions and Controversy Surround World Cup
hot_topics.synthesis: The World Cup is generating significant political attention and controversy, from European royals and leaders offering encouragement to their national teams, to Florida Governor Ron DeSantis's blunt assessment of the US team's performance. European Parliament members are demanding an investigation into FIFA President Gianni Infantino after a US player's red card suspension was overturned following a call from President Trump, raising concerns about ethical breaches and external influence on tournament rules. The event also highlights national pride, as seen in unified French political support for "Les Bleus" and the passionate Moroccan fan base in Brussels.

hot_topics.topic: Escalating Tensions in the Middle East
hot_topics.synthesis: A shaky ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran has temporarily paused two days of fighting, but concerns persist about Iran's potential to disrupt global oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, nine months into a U.S.-brokered ceasefire, Israeli forces have expanded their control to nearly 70% of Gaza, intensifying the humanitarian crisis for Palestinians. The conflict continues to have deadly consequences, exemplified by the killing of a prominent Palestinian aid worker in an Israeli strike, highlighting the fragile stability and ongoing human cost in the region.

hot_topics.topic: Donald Trump's Enduring Political Influence and Tactics
hot_topics.synthesis: Donald Trump continues to exert significant influence over U.S. politics and international affairs, from a federal judge reluctantly approving an SEC settlement with Elon Musk that stemmed from actions during the Trump administration, to a housing bill passing into law despite his refusal to sign it. Internationally, his intervention reportedly led to the overturning of a World Cup player's red card, drawing criticism from European lawmakers. Domestically, Trump is employing "communist" messaging against Democrats, while a new book analyzes his return to office as a fundamental transformation of the presidency marked by an expansion of executive power.

items.title: Lore of the rings
items.summary: Trees contain an extensive "archive" within their rings, holding information about planetary shifts, cosmic events, and historical developments. Scientists are increasingly unlocking these dendrochronological records to understand past environmental and global changes.

items.title: Allstate accuses Broadcom of auditing it because it quit VMware, CA
items.summary: Allstate Insurance has accused Broadcom of issuing "haphazard" license audits in retaliation for Allstate's decision not to renew contracts with VMware and CA Technologies. The allegations were made in relation to a lawsuit filed by VMware against Allstate regarding license compliance.

items.title: Flores Hobbits' eating habits offer clues about their evolutionary past
items.summary: New research suggests that "Flores Hobbits" (Homo floresiensis) likely scavenged kills from Komodo dragons rather than actively hunting pygmy elephants. This finding could revise assumptions about their intelligence and early hominin migration out of Africa.

items.title: Judge doesn't like Elon Musk settlement with SEC, but says court can't block it
items.summary: A federal judge reluctantly approved a $1.5 million settlement between Elon Musk and the SEC, despite having "significant misgivings" about the deal's leniency for a rule violation that allegedly harmed Twitter investors. The judge noted a high legal bar for rejecting the settlement, despite concerns about the SEC's decision-making during the Trump administration.

items.title: OpenAI may have made a fatal misstep in copyright fight with news orgs
items.summary: OpenAI is facing calls for "serious sanctions" from news organizations, including The New York Times, which accuse the AI firm of repeatedly lying and concealing evidence in a copyright infringement lawsuit. News organizations claim OpenAI hid logs that could show ChatGPT users skirting paywalls by regurgitating articles.

items.title: OpenAI wants its new tool to do your work for you and with you
items.summary: OpenAI has released "ChatGPT Work," a new tool designed to automate and manage complex tasks and entire workflows for hours or on a schedule, with user approval for key actions. It aims to solve previous limitations of short-lived automated tasks and allows for monitoring from mobile devices.

items.title: Volkswagen Group tells its board how to fix it, unions disagree
items.summary: Volkswagen Group's supervisory board rejected a plan to address declining profit margins, despite the company's strong EV sales in Europe. The decision highlights the significant influence of worker unions and the German state on the automaker's governance.

items.title: All the best looks from Paris Haute Couture Week 2026
items.summary: Paris Haute Couture Week 2026 showcased numerous fashion looks amidst scorching temperatures in the French capital. The event highlighted the latest trends and designs in high fashion.

items.title: Chip giant SK Hynix raises $26.5bn in mega US share sale
items.summary: South Korean chip giant SK Hynix has successfully raised $26.5 billion in a massive US share sale, marking the largest-ever debut by a foreign firm on the Nasdaq. The shares are scheduled to begin trading on Friday.

items.title: East Asia braces for destructive typhoon as landslides kill 15 in Philippines
items.summary: East Asia is bracing for Typhoon Bavi, a 1,000 km-wide storm forecast to be one of the strongest in decades, heading towards Taiwan and south-eastern China. This comes as landslides in the Philippines have already killed 15 people.

items.title: How an app in South Korea will let victims track their stalkers
items.summary: South Korea is introducing an app to allow stalking victims to track their stalkers, with officials hoping it will aid victims despite some experts questioning its potential impact. The tool aims to provide a new layer of protection and monitoring.

items.title: Huge crowds in Mashhad as Iran's late supreme leader is buried
items.summary: Huge crowds gathered in Mashhad for the burial of Iran's late supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, at the Imam Reza shrine, concluding six days of public mourning ceremonies. This event marks a significant moment for Iran's religious and political landscape.

items.title: Man fatally shot by ICE in Houston was not intended target, DHS says
items.summary: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) stated that a man fatally shot by ICE agents in Houston was not their intended target. Lorenzo Salgado Araujo was shot during a traffic stop while agents were searching for a different individual.

items.title: Man nearly sucked out of window mid-air on Ryanair plane, passengers say
items.summary: Passengers reported that a man was nearly sucked out of a window mid-air on a Ryanair-owned Malta Air flight, prompting medical treatment for the individual. Ryanair confirmed the incident, which occurred during the flight.

items.title: Nolan Wells' family demands answers about US teen's death after boating trip
items.summary: The family of US teen Nolan Wells is demanding answers regarding his death, after his body was found near Mississippi's Horn Island days after a boating trip with friends. The circumstances surrounding his death remain under investigation.

items.title: Palestinians mourn Gaza World Cup screenings organiser killed in Israeli strike
items.summary: Palestinians are mourning Mohammed al-Wahidi, an aid worker and organizer of Gaza World Cup screenings, who was killed in an Israeli strike. Wahidi had become a prominent humanitarian figure during the ongoing Israel-Hamas war.

items.title: Pressure builds on Europe's biggest port to be greener
items.summary: A lawsuit demands that the Port of Rotterdam moves faster to cut its dependence on fossil fuel firms.

items.title: US declassifies even more UFO videos
items.summary: The US government has declassified and released a fourth installment of unresolved UFO videos, promising to continue releasing more files on a rolling basis. This ongoing disclosure adds to the public record of unexplained aerial phenomena.

items.title: We've saved £6,000 on holidays by swapping homes with strangers
items.summary: The BBC reports on the growing trend of home swapping, with individuals saving substantial amounts of money, such as £6,000, on holidays by exchanging homes with strangers. This method allows travelers to reduce accommodation costs significantly.

items.title: What's next for Iran after the funeral of their late supreme leader?
items.summary: Following the funeral of Iran's late supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, attention is turning to how Iran's new leadership will address various national issues. This transition is expected to shape the country's future policies and international relations.

items.title: AI-generated videos to maximally drive a target brain region
items.summary: Researchers are developing AI-generated videos specifically designed to maximally stimulate a target brain region. This project, Nevo, explores how visual stimuli can be optimized to elicit specific neural responses.

items.title: EU Commission: addictive design Instagram and Facebook in breach of the DSA
items.summary: The European Commission has declared that the addictive design features of Instagram and Facebook are in breach of the Digital Services Act (DSA). This ruling addresses concerns about platforms exploiting user vulnerabilities through elements like infinite scroll and autoplay.

items.title: GPT-5.6
items.summary: OpenAI has released GPT-5.6, its latest flagship AI model, available in three sizes: Luna, Terra, and Sol, with significant improvements in long-running agentic performance. These models feature a million-token context window and new API capabilities like programmatic tool calling and multi-agent support.

items.title: Hy3
items.summary: Tencent has introduced Hy3, a new AI research project. The article provides details on the development and capabilities of this model.

items.title: Interview with Mitchell Hashimoto about Ghostty and Zig
items.summary: An interview with Mitchell Hashimoto discusses his work on Ghostty, a terminal emulator, and his use of the Zig programming language. The conversation delves into the development process and the advantages of Zig for systems programming.

items.title: Late Bronze Age Collapse
items.summary: An article provides a brief introduction to the Late Bronze Age Collapse, a historical period marked by widespread societal upheaval and the decline of major civilizations. It explores the various theories and factors contributing to this significant historical event.

items.title: Write code like a human will maintain it
items.summary: An article argues for writing code that is easily maintainable by humans, emphasizing clarity and readability over overly complex or clever solutions. This approach aims to reduce future development and debugging challenges.

items.title: 9 months into a ceasefire, Israel now controls nearly 70% of Gaza
items.summary: Nine months after a U.S.-brokered ceasefire, Israel now controls nearly 70% of Gaza, having pushed deeper into the territory and causing a deadly toll for Palestinians. This expansion highlights the shifting dynamics and ongoing humanitarian crisis in the region.

items.title: A major housing bill is set to become law at midnight — even though Trump says he won't sign
items.summary: A major housing bill is set to become law at midnight without President Trump's signature, as he refused to sign it unless Congress first passed his sweeping voter ID bill. This demonstrates a legislative override of presidential opposition on a key policy issue.

items.title: Count Binface: The intergalactic warrior who could upend Britain's strangest election
items.summary: "Count Binface," an intergalactic warrior candidate, is challenging Nigel Farage in Britain's "strangest election," with questions about the Reform UK leader's finances overshadowing his comeback. Binface's campaign injects an unusual element into the political landscape.

items.title: No internet, no screen time? FCC weighs cutting subsidy that lowers school internet bills
items.summary: The FCC is considering ending a federal subsidy, known as E-rate, which helps schools cut internet costs, a move advocated by FCC Chairman Brendan Carr before his appointment by Donald Trump. This decision could impact many schools relying on the discount.

items.title: One of Spain's deadliest wildfires kills at least 12 people, with 23 others missing
items.summary: A wildfire in Spain's southern province of Almeria, a popular holiday destination, has killed at least 12 people, with 23 others missing. Many victims were found in burnt vehicles, suggesting they died while attempting to escape.

items.title: One U.S. visa program is growing rapidly. No one is happy with it
items.summary: A rapidly growing US visa program for farm laborers, H-2A, is facing widespread dissatisfaction, despite Republican calls for its expansion as part of immigration reform. Obstacles persist in finding common ground for changes to the program.

items.title: Trump leans on 'communist' messaging as economic angst drives Democrats
items.summary: Donald Trump is intensifying his "communist" messaging against Democrats, particularly focusing on economic angst to appeal to voters. This rhetorical strategy echoes past campaigns where he used similar labels.

items.title: U.S.-Iran fighting appears to pause. And, life inside Israel's military zones in Gaza
items.summary: Fighting between the U.S. and Iran has seemingly paused after two days of clashes, amidst a fragile ceasefire agreement. Simultaneously, Israel has expanded its control to nearly 70% of Gaza, nine months into a ceasefire, leading to further displacement and casualties for Palestinians.

items.title: Waymo called the cops on teen riders, raising privacy concerns
items.summary: Waymo alerted police and disabled a driverless taxi after two 15-year-olds were allegedly drinking alcohol and shooting toy guns from inside the vehicle. This incident raises privacy concerns about autonomous vehicle companies monitoring and intervening with passengers.

items.title: 'They got their rear end kicked,' DeSantis says of US team
items.summary: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis, while guest hosting a radio show, gave a blunt assessment of the US national team's 4-1 loss to Belgium in the World Cup, stating "they got their rear end kicked." Despite not being a soccer fan, he did attend a previous match.

items.title: Belgium's Red Devils get royal pep talk
items.summary: Belgium's King Philippe offered encouragement to the Red Devils, joining them for dinner before their World Cup quarterfinal against Spain, telling them "You must win." He joins a growing list of European royals attending the tournament.

items.title: Brussels wants action over Trump-Infantino red card controversy
items.summary: Seventy-two members of the European Parliament are demanding an investigation into FIFA President Gianni Infantino after FIFA overturned a red card suspension for a US striker following a call from President Donald Trump. Lawmakers accuse FIFA of a "disgraceful" perversion of justice and surrendering to the Trump administration.

items.title: Dream lives for Moroccans in Brussels
items.summary: Moroccan football fans in Brussels created an electric atmosphere at a tea salon, erupting in cheers for defensive plays and a missed French penalty during the World Cup quarterfinal against France. Supporters also expressed hopes to avoid violence after the match, recalling past unrest.

items.title: Emmanuel Macron cheers Les Bleus. So does Jordan Bardella.
items.summary: French politicians from across the spectrum, including President Emmanuel Macron and far-right leader Jordan Bardella, praised France's World Cup quarterfinal victory over Morocco, showcasing a rare moment of unity. They expressed pride in "Les Bleus" as the team advanced to the semifinals.

items.title: We Know Simple Fluids Can Flow. Turns Out, Some Can Fracture.
items.summary: Researchers studying thick, viscous liquids like polypropylene have discovered that under certain conditions, these simple fluids can fracture rather than merely flow. This unexpected property challenges traditional understanding of fluid mechanics.

items.title: Introducing Muse Spark 1.1
items.summary: Meta has released Muse Spark 1.1, an updated AI model now available via an API, which claims substantial improvements in agentic tool calling and computer use. This release follows the initial Muse Spark model from April and includes new evaluation reports detailing its capabilities.

items.title: llm 0.31.1
items.summary: Version 0.31.1 of the `llm` command-line tool has been released, fixing a bug in OpenAI Chat Completion endpoints related to tool calls with empty arguments. This update improves compatibility and stability with various AI providers.

items.title: llm-meta-ai 0.1
items.summary: The `llm-meta-ai 0.1` plugin has been released, enabling users of the LLM command-line tool to run prompts against Meta's new `muse-spark-1.1` model. This provides command-line and Python library access to the model.

items.title: Quoting OpenAI
items.summary: OpenAI is attempting to clarify the functionality of its new ChatGPT Work tool, particularly regarding data handling for cloud versus desktop app usage. The company states cloud-based work runs remotely, while desktop app work can access local files and apps, with local threads remaining on the user's computer.

items.title: The new GPT-5.6 family: Luna, Terra, Sol
items.summary: OpenAI has launched the GPT-5.6 model family—Luna, Terra, and Sol—which significantly outperforms Claude Fable 5 in long-running agentic workflows, despite facing challenges in benchmarks like SWE-Bench Pro. The models offer a large context window, new API features like programmatic tool calling and multi-agent capabilities, and varied pricing structures.

items.title: The promise and peril of an abundance faction
items.summary: An analysis suggests that while the YIMBY movement has achieved considerable success in passing housing legislation, the broader "abundance" policy concept is less developed beyond housing and energy. It also notes the ongoing challenge for Democrats to appeal to working-class voters beyond specific successful candidates.

items.title: After Apple, India’s smartphone manufacturing boom enters new phase with Vivo JV
items.summary: India's smartphone manufacturing sector is entering a new phase with a joint venture involving Vivo, following Apple's success, potentially establishing a template for other Chinese smartphone makers in the country. This indicates a growing trend of local production in India.

items.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
items.summary: Lyzr, a startup developing AI agents for enterprises, successfully raised a $100 million funding round by utilizing its own AI agent to manage the fundraise. This demonstrates the practical application and efficacy of their product.

items.title: Can AI answer the $3 trillion question?
items.summary: The debate over the return on investment (ROI) for Artificial Intelligence has re-emerged with even larger financial figures, prompting questions about the potential consequences of AI's economic impact. The discussion centers on whether AI can deliver on its promise of significant value.

items.title: Charles Hudson shares the common mistakes he’s seen after investing in 500+ startups
items.summary: Charles Hudson, a venture capitalist from Precursor Ventures, shared insights on common mistakes made by early-stage founders after investing in over 500 startups. He discussed the current challenges faced by founders and key pitfalls to avoid for securing funding.

items.title: Dumb Co dared me to trade my iPhone for a hacked flip phone
items.summary: Dumb Co sells flip phones that can sync with smartphones, aiming to blend the extensive connectivity of modern devices with the simplistic experience of early 2000s phones. The product offers a unique compromise between digital detox and staying connected.

items.title: Elon Musk praises Mythos/Fable, promises not to ‘cut off’ Anthropic
items.summary: Elon Musk has publicly praised Mythos/Fable and assured that Anthropic, a competing AI company, would not be "cut off" from his platforms. This statement addresses concerns about fair competition and model hosting amidst significant revenue stakes.

items.title: EU threatens Meta with fines over addictive features on Facebook and Instagram
items.summary: The European Commission is threatening Meta with fines, asserting that addictive features on Facebook and Instagram, such as infinite scroll and personalized algorithms, violate the Digital Services Act (DSA). The EU warns these features breach regulations designed to protect user well-being.

items.title: FanDuel sent a video from star athlete Bryce Harper to a customer with a gambling problem
items.summary: FanDuel sent a promotional video from star athlete Bryce Harper to a customer known to have a gambling problem, raising ethical concerns about the relationship between professional athletes and gambling apps. This incident highlights the murky boundaries in sports betting marketing.

items.title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s No. 2 role
items.summary: Fidji Simo has stepped down from her full-time role as OpenAI's No. 2 executive due to an extended medical leave, creating a leadership vacuum. This departure comes at a critical time as OpenAI considers a possible IPO and competes in the enterprise market.

items.title: Florida ransomware negotiator convicted for helping ransomware gang extort US companies
items.summary: A Florida ransomware negotiator has been convicted and jailed for assisting a notorious ransomware group in extorting American companies. This marks the third such conviction, highlighting legal crackdowns on individuals who facilitate cybercrime.

items.title: Mercor is in talks for a $20B valuation
items.summary: Mercor is reportedly in discussions for a new valuation of $20 billion, which would be a significant increase from its $10 billion valuation just eight months prior. This indicates rapid growth and investor confidence in the company.

items.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
items.summary: Meta has introduced Muse Spark 1.1, its new AI model designed for coding, entering the competitive AI coding market. Muse Spark aims to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and assist with major code migrations, offering automation solutions for enterprises.

items.title: Netflix could be planning ‘always-on’ live TV channels
items.summary: Netflix is reportedly considering launching "always-on" live TV channels to combat slowing engagement and offer subscribers a continuous viewing option. This potential move would introduce a more traditional television experience to the streaming platform.

items.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
items.summary: The New York Times alleges that OpenAI deliberately concealed evidence, including tools and datasets, that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs during their copyright infringement lawsuit. This has led to an escalated motion for sanctions against OpenAI.

items.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
items.summary: OpenAI is discontinuing its AI-powered Atlas web browser less than a year after its launch, but plans to integrate some of its agentic browsing features into its desktop app and a Chrome extension. This indicates a shift in its strategy for AI-driven web interaction.

items.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
items.summary: OpenAI has stated that its GPT 5.6 models are the "preferred model" for Microsoft Copilot 365, amidst rumors of a potential split between the two companies. This affirms the continued integration of OpenAI's AI into Microsoft's productivity suite.

items.title: Oratomic raises $300M to build a viable quantum computer that needs only 20K qubits
items.summary: Oratomic has raised $300 million in funding to develop a viable quantum computer, claiming it will require only 20,000 qubits. The substantial investment was co-led by ARCH Venture Partners, Spark Capital, and Khosla Ventures.

items.title: Slate Auto teams up with Crayola to color its EV truck
items.summary: Slate Auto has partnered with Crayola to offer a range of bright crayon colors for its EV truck, providing owners with customizable and vibrant exterior options. This collaboration aims to differentiate the electric vehicle in the market.

items.title: AI Isn’t Human. Stop Talking About It Like It Is.
items.summary: An article criticizes the anthropomorphization of AI, particularly in discussions about models like Anthropic's Claude "silently performing reasoning steps." It clarifies that this refers to the AI deploying internal code to answer prompts without human-readable words, not actual inner thoughts or feelings.

items.title: Christopher Caldwell: Inside Donald Trump’s Ruthless Return
items.summary: Christopher Caldwell reviews "Regime Change," a new book on Donald Trump's return to office, which argues it marks a fundamental transformation of the presidency through expanded executive power. Caldwell notes the book's nuance in portraying Trump's complex and often contradictory impact on American public life.

items.title: Niall Ferguson: Can Iran Hold the Strait of Hormuz Hostage Forever?
items.summary: Niall Ferguson discusses the fragile state of the Strait of Hormuz, questioning whether the ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran will hold and if Iran can indefinitely threaten global oil shipping. His analysis comes amidst renewed strikes between the two nations.

items.title: Why the Charlie Kirk Conspiracy Theories Will Never Die
items.summary: Despite overwhelming evidence presented in a preliminary hearing, conspiracy theories surrounding the assassination of Charlie Kirk persist. The legal proceedings detailed a confession, DNA evidence, and surveillance footage linking the accused killer, Tyler Robinson, to the crime.

items.title: ‘Choose the Girl No Matter What’
items.summary: A 15-year-old boy, Owen Poirier, died by suicide after being suspended from school and facing a criminal investigation over an allegation of inappropriate touching on a bus. His parents express profound regret, wishing they had known the depth of his despair.

items.title: Anker’s 3-in-1 Qi2.2 charging station is $95 off
items.summary: Anker's Prime Wireless Charging Station, a 3-in-1 device for iPhone, Apple Watch, and AirPods, is available at a discounted price of $134.99. It features Qi2.2 compatibility for up to 25W iPhone charging, a touchscreen display, and adjustable phone stand.

items.title: Disney Plus is reportedly looking into a free streaming tier
items.summary: Disney Plus is reportedly exploring the possibility of introducing a free, ad-supported streaming tier for some of its content. This move is part of ongoing discussions to better serve fans and potentially compete with platforms like YouTube.

items.title: Doom’s new expansion shows just what makes id Software special
items.summary: Id Software launched "Revelations," a new expansion for Doom: The Dark Ages, which adds a powerful weapon and more demonic levels, showcasing the studio's distinctive fast-paced shooter action. The launch was overshadowed by recent Xbox mass layoffs.

items.title: Empathy for the optimizers
items.summary:
This is
Optimizer
, a weekly newsletter sent from
Verge
senior reviewer
Victoria Song
that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. Opt in for
Optimizer
here
.
Bryan Johnson, best known as the man who wants to live forever…

items.title: I spent a week using the Trump phone — it sucks
items.summary: A review of the Trump phone (T1) concludes it is not a serious device, citing its initial vague announcements, lack of US manufacturing, and ultimately poor performance. The $499 device is deemed a functional but substandard product.

items.title: Instagram’s Adam Mosseri: If you don’t like AI, ‘then you shouldn’t have it in your feed’
items.summary: Instagram head Adam Mosseri stated that while AI content should not be filtered out entirely, users should be informed if content is AI-generated and have control over whether it appears in their feeds. He suggested that users who enjoy AI content should have the option for an "AI town" feed.

items.title: Polestar owners left ‘holding the bag’ after EV brand pulls out of the US
items.summary: Polestar's decision to exit the US market, following a federal ban on vehicles with Chinese-made connected software, has left thousands of owners and dealers uncertain about future service and warranty support. The EV company, majority-owned by China's Geely, will stop selling cars in the US from the 2027 model year.

items.title: Xreal’s new AR glasses are way cheaper and almost just right
items.summary: Xreal's new A01 Plus AR glasses offer a cheaper, lighter alternative to previous models at $299, with surprisingly bright and contrast-rich screens. Despite a flimsy frame, they provide a comfortable viewing experience.
